resources.string: A resizable weather widget showing current conditions and forecast
Choose how to show forecast vs actual comparison for past dates
Forecast bar - Yellow line showing predicted range (Recommended)
Choose which weather API to prefer (fallback to other if unavailable)
Alternate - Switch between NWS and Open-Meteo
Prefer NWS - National Weather Service (US only)
View recent API calls and troubleshoot issues
30-day accuracy summary for both weather sources

The widget adapts to different sizes:

• 1x1: Forecast high for today (+ current temp if space allows)
• 1x3: Yesterday, today, tomorrow (text only)
• 2x3: Same as 1x3 but with graphical temperature bars
• 4+ columns: Additional forecast days (4 cols = 2 forecast days, 5 cols = 3 forecast days, etc.)

Resize the widget by long-pressing it on your home screen and dragging the edges.
The large temperature in the top-left corner shows the current temperature, interpolated from hourly forecast data for smooth transitions. Updates 1–4 times per hour based on temperature change rate.

Tap the API indicator in the top-right corner to toggle between weather sources:

• NWS: National Weather Service (US only)
• Meteo: Open-Meteo (global coverage)

You can set your preferred source in Settings (Alternate, Prefer NWS, or Prefer Open-Meteo).
Use the left/right arrows to browse up to 30 days of historical weather data and future forecasts. Past days show actual temperatures compared to predictions for accuracy analysis.

The app compares 1-day-ahead forecasts against actual weather. Five display modes available in Settings:

• FORECAST_BAR (default): Yellow bar overlay showing predicted range alongside actual temperature bar
• ACCURACY_DOT: Colored indicator (green ≤2°, yellow ≤5°, red >5° error)
• SIDE_BY_SIDE: Shows "72° (N:68°)" with source abbreviation
• DIFFERENCE: Shows "72° (N:+4)" with temperature difference
• NONE: No forecast comparison

View detailed 30-day statistics in the Statistics screen.

Access Settings to customize:

• Accuracy Display Mode (how forecast comparisons are shown)
• API Preference (which weather source to use)
• View Detailed Statistics (30-day accuracy breakdown)
• API Diagnostics (troubleshoot data fetching issues)

Open Settings by long-pressing the widget and selecting "Configure".

Update frequency scales based on battery level:

• Plugged in: Every 30 minutes
• Battery >50%%: Every 1 hour
• Battery 20–50%%: Every 2 hours
• Battery <20%%: Every 4 hours

Manual refresh is always available by tapping the widget.

Choose your location method when configuring the widget:

• GPS: Automatic location detection
• ZIP Code: Manual entry for specific location
• Default: Falls back to Google HQ (37.4220, -122.0841) if location unavailable

The widget displays the location name at the bottom.
